user_story: As a collection manager, I want to authenticate catalogers and enforce role‑based access control for collection and item metadata, so that I can restrict who can create, edit, or delete records in my collection.
true_user_story: As a collection manager or cataloger I want to be able to see who last edited a record and when.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to be able to assign and revoke user roles (e.g., cataloger, viewer, editor) for each collection and item, so that I can enforce fine‑grained access control and ensure that only authorized users can view or edit metadata while also tracking who made changes and when.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict who has create/update/delete access to records in my collection.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to assign cataloger roles to users for specific collections, so that I can control who can create, update, or delete items within those collections.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict who has edit access to individual records in my collection.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to enforce that each authenticated user can be a member of at most one collection, so that we can maintain clear accountability and simplify permission management.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict access to collection metadata.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to create, retrieve, update, and delete items and their metadata records within a collection, so that I can keep the collection accurate and up‑to‑date.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to edit an arbitrary number of Items at once so that I can make changes to all Items in a collection efficiently.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to verify items, so that I can confirm that each item's metadata and components meet the required quality and completeness standards before it is published or further edited.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to use a grid/spreadsheet style interface so I can edit Items quickly.

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want the system to enforce that a record can be saved only when it contains an identifier, and can be published only when it contains a Title, Identifier, Type, and all fields required by its assigned profile (including structural metadata when the item has components), so that incomplete or invalid records are not persisted or made public, ensuring the repository’s data integrity and quality.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to save a record without all of the required metadata so that I can start a record with the metadata I have and come back to finish it later.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to save an item as a draft and later publish it, so that I can work on metadata incrementally without exposing incomplete records.
true_user_story: As a user I want to be able to save a record only when the record has an identifier so that we don't end up with a bunch of empty records in the collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to save a record as a draft and publish it only when it has an identifier and all required metadata, so that incomplete records are not exposed to users and the repository remains clean.
true_user_story: As a user I want to be able to publish records only when the record has a Title, Identifier, Type, and

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to filter items by any Duke Core metadata field and by status (e.g., incomplete, verified, published) across all collections, so that I can quickly identify the records that need my attention and update them efficiently.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want a list of all of the records I have access to so I can get to work quickly.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to be able to search and filter collection records by Duke Core fields, status, and production stage, so that I can quickly monitor progress, identify items or collections with issues, and report problems to the Digital Production Manager.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to search for records based on status so I can track the progress of a collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to filter search results to show only incomplete items in collections I have access to, so that I can quickly identify records that need my attention.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to search for records based on all Duke Core fields.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to search for and filter collections by their metadata profile, the status of the items they contain, and the categories that belong to them, so that I can quickly identify collections that need attention and manage them efficiently.
true_user_story: As an end user I want to be able to search across all collections at once.

user_story: As a collections manager, I want to be able to configure Duke Core metadata fields as optional, required, or repeatable in the collection settings, so that my collection can enforce the Duke Core schema while allowing flexibility for metadata entry.
true_user_story: As a cataloger and a collections manager I want Trident to support Duke Core so that metadata is consistent between collections.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want the DMR editing screen to enforce Duke Core authority lists for each metadata field, validate entries against those lists, and provide on‑screen guidance, so that I can create consistent, accurate metadata that meets collection standards and can be easily monitored by the collections manager.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want required DC fields to be enforced so that all records have values for all required DC fields. ♦

user_story: As a collections manager, I want to assign a Duke Core metadata profile to a collection and enforce its required fields while allowing optional fields to be used at the collection’s discretion, so that all items in the collection are validated against the core schema and consistent across collections.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want to be able to repeat repeatable DC Fields so that I can enter all of the relevant metadata. ♦

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to create and edit DMRs using Duke Core authority lists with built‑in validation and guidance, and to view incomplete items and assign catalogers to collections and items, so that I can maintain consistent, complete metadata across all collections.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want Optional DC fields to be available but not required so that my collection can decide to use them or not. ♦

user_story: As a cataloger, I want the DMR editing screen to automatically pull the appropriate external authority lists for each metadata field defined in the collection’s metadata profile, validate entered values against those lists, and provide contextual guidance, so that I can create accurate, compliant records efficiently.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to be able to create controlled vocabularies so that they can be used as authority lists during metadata creation.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want the DMR editing screen to automatically suggest and validate the appropriate authority lists for each metadata field, so that I can ensure consistent, accurate, and compliant metadata entry without having to manually search for the correct controlled vocabularies.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want authority lists to be easily accessible and contextually relevant so it is easy to use the authority.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want the DMR editing screen to display context‑sensitive authority lists for each metadata field, validate the record against the collection’s metadata profile, and provide real‑time guidance for incomplete or incorrect entries, so that I can efficiently create accurate metadata records with minimal errors.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to use external authority lists so I they can be used during metadata creation.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to view incomplete items and preview all media components (audio/video) inline on the create/update screen, so that I can verify and complete metadata records efficiently without leaving the form.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to see/hear/watch/read the digital object I am cataloging so I don't have to move between multiple applications while I'm creating metadata.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to create and update items, components, and scanning specifications in a single interface, so that I can view all metadata and the digital object while editing without leaving the application.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to see/hear/watch/read all components associated on the with an item on the create/update screen so I don't have to leave the form.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be able to view incomplete items, monitor collection progress, and see the digital object and all its components directly within the create/update interface, so that I can efficiently complete metadata tasks, verify accuracy, and maintain context without leaving the form.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to see all of the metadata for a record while I'm editing it so that I have the full context for the item I'm cataloging.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want the system to let me flag individual items and collections with a dedicated ‘problem’ status and to view a consolidated report of all flagged records, so that I can monitor collection progress, quickly identify and resolve issues, and keep the project on track.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want each record to have a status so I can track the progress of the records in the collection.

user_story: As a digital production manager, I want to view and update the status of each item in the collection and flag items that have reported problems, so that I can monitor collection progress, quickly identify issues, and keep the project on schedule.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want statuses to include a status that flags a record as having a problem so we can track issues as the arise.

user_story: As a content editor, I want Trident to support UTF‑8 encoded component files, metadata, and derivatives—complete with a UI that respects each user role’s permissions—so that I can manage internationalised content without encountering encoding errors or permission problems.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want Trident to support the UTF-8 character set so that I can use any character necessary when creating metadata.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to validate and edit descriptive metadata records (DMRs) using the appropriate authority lists and metadata profiles, so that every item—especially those with one or more components—has accurate descriptive metadata and an associated structural metadata record.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to map fields in inherited metadata to Duke Core fields and import a spreadsheet and create records.

user_story: As a metadata editor, I want the DMR editing screen to validate each descriptive metadata record against its Metadata Profile, automatically apply the appropriate authority lists to the relevant fields, and prompt me to create a structural metadata record whenever I add components, so that I can ingest metadata accurately, maintain consistency, and provide clear guidance to catalogers.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to import metadata and create records from a DCP digitazation guide.

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to define and maintain bidirectional relationships (e.g., isPartOf, hasPart, hasFeaturedItem, isMemberOfCollection) between digital items—such as images, multi‑images, books, pages, text, and media—and their corresponding physical context or external resources via pointers (URIs), so that the catalog can present a discoverable, integrated view of the collection that allows end users to navigate from a digital item to its parent collection, related media, or physical folder (EAD) seamlessly.
true_user_story: As a Archivist I want bi-directional linking between items in the digital collections and EAD finding aids

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to add a pointer component to each book item that links to its corresponding EAD finding aid, so that the collection is integrated with archival metadata and users can navigate seamlessly between the digital collection and the archival description.
true_user_story: As a End User I want to be able to see the physical context of an Item in a digital collection (i.e. See inside this folder; EAD)

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to create and publish collections that include bi‑directional links between digital items and external EAD finding aids, so that users can seamlessly navigate between the digital representation and the physical archival context while I can monitor the progress of these integrations and manage cataloger permissions.
true_user_story: As a Collection Manager I want my collection to be discoverable in the Catalog

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager (DPU), I want to group individual components into logical items, so that I can streamline the digitization workflow and maintain an organized, traceable set of digital assets.
true_user_story: As a Digital Production User (DPU) I want to be able to create Components.

user_story: As a DPU, I want to add component files to Trident and have it automatically create administrative metadata and derivatives for each component, so that I can efficiently manage the component lifecycle and provide access in multiple formats without manual intervention.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create Technical Metadata with for Component.

user_story: As a DPU, I want Trident to automatically add component files, create administrative metadata, generate component derivatives, associate each component with a descriptive metadata record and a metadata profile, and create a structural metadata record for items containing components, so that the item is fully described and ready for quality verification by the DPD.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create Preservation Metadata for a Component.

user_story: As a DPU, I want to add component files to Trident and automatically generate administrative metadata and component derivatives, so that I can reduce manual entry, ensure components are properly catalogued, and have them ready for scanning and quality checks.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to upload master surrogates to a Component.

user_story: As a Digital Production User (DPU), I want the system to automatically generate a structural metadata record whenever an item is created with one or more components, so that the item can be properly navigated and displayed without manual intervention.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to add one or more Components to an Item.

user_story: As a Digital Production Developer (DPD), I want to automatically generate a structural metadata record for any item that contains components and link each component to a Descriptive Metadata Record (DMR) associated with a Metadata Profile, so that the item meets metadata compliance and each component is properly described for downstream processing.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create structural metadata for an Item so I can define the sequence of components.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to assign scanner operators to specific component groups and monitor their progress, so that the digitization workflow is organized, components are scanned in the correct order, and quality standards are consistently met.
true_user_story: As a Digital Production Developer (DPD) I want to group components that require digitization so that I can create "digitizable units".

user_story: As a Scanner Operator, I want to scan materials and add components to an item, so that the item’s technical metadata is complete and ready for downstream processing.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to check-out/check-in groups of Components.

user_story: As a Scanner Operator, I want to create and apply scanning specifications for new materials, so that the digitized components are accurate and their technical metadata passes validation rules.
true_user_story: As a DPD I want to verify the correctness and completeness of components so that I can ensure quality standards are met.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to create a new component for an item, so that I can include it in the digitization workflow.
true_user_story: As a DPD I want to capture digitization statistics so that I can monitor Operator efficiency and make projections for future projects.

user_story: As a Digital Production User (DPU), I want to create component derivatives, so that I can produce alternate format outputs for distribution.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want Trident to create derivatives for me when I upload a Master.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want a real‑time dashboard that displays the number of items published, the number of items digitized, and the status of master‑to‑derivative relationships, so that I can monitor collection progress, verify metadata, and quickly report any problems with items or collections.
true_user_story: As a User I want to monitor the progress of a digital collection project.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to track the status of each component within an item (e.g., scanned, quality‑checked, described), so that I can monitor progress and identify bottlenecks in the digitization workflow.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many Items have been digitized.♦

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to assign scanner operators to specific items and record quality‑control assessments for each scan, so that the digitization workflow is streamlined and each scan meets the required quality standards.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many digitized Items have been described.♦

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to view incomplete items, monitor collection progress, and report problems with items and collections, so that I can ensure the collection is completed accurately and on time.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many Items have been published.♦

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to view a real‑time dashboard that displays the number of items in each collection that are digitized, described, and have their master‑derivative relationships established, so that I can monitor project progress, identify bottlenecks, and allocate resources efficiently.
true_user_story: As Collection Manager or Sponsor I want to describe the collection.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to define authority lists for metadata fields, create and validate DMRs, set up relationships between items, and manage scanning specifications and scanner operators, so that I can maintain consistent, accurate metadata, streamline the digitization workflow, and monitor the progress of the digital collection project.
true_user_story: As a Preservation Librarian I want the relationship between masters and derivatives to be managed by the system so I don't have to.

user_story: As a Metadata Librarian, I want to assign a metadata application profile to a collection, so that all items and categories within that collection are automatically validated against the profile’s required fields and rules.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want to define the metadata schema for a collection.

user_story: As a Metadata Librarian, I want to configure the authority list that applies to each metadata field in a collection’s profile, so that the DMR editing screen can enforce validation rules and provide clear guidance to catalogers when they enter or edit metadata.
true_user_story: As a Metadata librarian I want to define Authorities to be used for specific fields in a collection MD Schema.

user_story: As a Metadata Librarian, I want to build a DMR editing screen that automatically applies indexing rules and authority list checks, validates the metadata against defined rules, and displays clear guidance to the cataloger, so that submitted metadata is consistently valid and catalogers can correct errors efficiently.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want to define validation rules for specific fields in a collection.

user_story: As a Metadata Librarian, I want to define and enforce validation rules and indexing rules for each metadata field, specify the authority lists that apply to those fields, and have these rules evaluated in a DMR editing screen that displays the validated DMR to the end user while providing real‑time guidance to the cataloger, so that I can guarantee that all descriptive metadata conforms to the collection’s application profile and is immediately usable by both catalogers and end users.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want validation rules enforced when MD is submitted.
